back_translation: Now Boaz went up to the city gate and sat down there. Behold, the kinsman-redeemer of whom Boaz had spoken came walking by. Boaz said, "Friend, please come over and sit here." So he came over and sat down.
Then Boaz called ten of the elders of the city and said, "Please sit here." So they sat down.
Boaz then said to the kinsman-redeemer, "Naomi, who has returned from the land of Moab, is about to sell the portion of land that belonged to Elimelech our relative.

I therefore thought I should inform you: 'Please buy that piece of land in the presence of those sitting here and in the presence of the elders of my people.' If you will redeem it, please do so. But if you do not desire to redeem it, please tell me so I may know, for besides you no one has the right to redeem, and I am next in line." He answered, "I will redeem it."

Then Boaz said, "On the day you buy the land from Naomi and from Ruth the Moabite woman, you must also receive her, the widow of the deceased, in order to continue the name of the deceased through his inheritance."
The kinsman-redeemer replied, "I cannot redeem it, lest I damage my own inheritance. Please take my right of redemption for yourself, for I cannot redeem."

In former times in Israel, concerning redemption and exchange of property, to make every matter binding, one would remove his sandal and give it to the other party — this was the method of confirmation in Israel.
So the kinsman-redeemer said to Boaz, "Buy it for yourself," and then he removed his sandal.
At that, Boaz said to the elders and all the people, "You are witnesses today that I have purchased everything that belonged to Elimelech, Chilion, and Mahlon from Naomi.

Moreover, I have received Ruth the Moabite woman, the widow of Mahlon, as my wife, in order to continue the name of the deceased through his inheritance, so that the name of the deceased will not be cut off from his brothers and from the gate of the place where he lived. You are witnesses today."

The elders and all the people at the city gate replied, "We are witnesses. May the LORD make this woman who is entering your house like Rachel and Leah, the two who built up the lineage of Israel. May you prosper in Ephrathah and have a name in Bethlehem.
And may your house be like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah, through the offspring that the LORD will give you by this young woman."
So Boaz received Ruth, and she became his wife. When he was together with her, the LORD enabled her to conceive, and she gave birth to a son.
The women of the city said to Naomi, "May the LORD be praised, who today has not abandoned you without a kinsman-redeemer. May his name be famous in Israel.
This son will be one who restores your life and will care for you in your old age. For your daughter-in-law who loves you and who is better to you than seven sons has given birth to him."
Naomi then took the child into her lap and became a nurse to him.
The neighboring women named the child, saying, "A son has been born to Naomi!" So they called him Obed. He was the father of Jesse, and Jesse was the father of David.
The following are the descendants of Perez — Perez was the father of Hezron.
Hezron was the father of Ram, Ram was the father of Amminadab.
Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
Salmon was the father of Boaz, Boaz was the father of Obed.
Obed was the father of Jesse, and Jesse was the father of David.
